title: Silky Salty Argentinian Lamb Bake
description: A silky, savory lamb bake infused with Argentine spices. Perfect for a low‑carb brunch.
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F). Grease a 20x30 cm baking dish with a thin layer of olive oil.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat 1 tbsp olive oil. Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook another minute, until fragrant.
Increase heat to medium‑high, add lamb mince. Break up with a spoon and cook until browned, about 6–8 minutes.
Stir in smoked paprika, cumin, oregano, parsley, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes, letting the spices bloom.
Pour in heavy cream, stir to combine. Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 5 minutes, allowing the mixture to thicken slightly.
Transfer the mixture to the prepared baking dish and spread evenly.
Sprinkle grated Parmesan over the top, then drizzle the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil.
Bake uncovered for 20–25 minutes, until the top is golden and the edges are bubbling.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes before slicing. Serve hot, optionally with a side of sautéed spinach or a low‑carb salad.
